negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: No audited repo implements SynchronousScheduler one-time singleton initialization with instance() failing before initialize(), a single successful initialize(address), and repeated initialize returning already-initialized.
related_but_insufficient_code: The port has stateless get_scheduler_address and per-add-on scheduler_address configuration, plus payload-time Scheduler storage scanning, but no OnceLock-backed scheduler singleton lifecycle.

notes: Confirmed gap for old scheduling service lifecycle invariant.
rationale: The nodebuilder singleton candidate states the intended one-time global scheduler API, and the later internal fix makes initialize() and instance() share the same OnceLock-backed instance.
statement: SynchronousScheduler must be initialized at most once with a scheduler contract address, and after successful initialization SynchronousScheduler::instance() must return that same initialized scheduler; access before initialization must fail and repeated initialization must return an error.
suggested_falsification_tests: In an isolated process, assert instance() fails before initialization, initialize(address) succeeds once, instance() returns a scheduler with that address, and a second initialize(address) returns the already-initialized error.
summary: SynchronousScheduler singleton initialization is not preserved.
test_or_falsification_candidate: API parity test for SynchronousScheduler::instance before/after initialize cannot be expressed because the type/surface is absent.
title: SynchronousScheduler singleton is initialized once
